data.label: Conversational Retrieval QA Chain
data.name: conversationalRetrievalQAChain
data.description: Document QA - built on RetrievalQAChain to provide a chat history component

inputParams.label: Rephrase Prompt
inputParams.name: rephrasePrompt
inputParams.description: Using previous chat history, rephrase question into a standalone question
inputParams.warning: Prompt must include input variables: {chat_history} and {question}

inputParams.label: Response Prompt
inputParams.name: responsePrompt
inputParams.description: Taking the rephrased question, search for answer from the provided context
inputParams.default:
I want you to act as a document that I am having a conversation with. Your name is "AI Assistant". Using the provided context, answer the user's question to the best of your ability using the resources provided.
If there is nothing in the context relevant to the question at hand, just say "Hmm, I'm not sure" and stop after that. Refuse to answer any question not about the info. Never break character.
------------
{context}
------------
REMEMBER: If there is no relevant information within the context, just say "Hmm, I'm not sure". Don't try to make up an answer. Never break character.

inputAnchors.label: Memory
inputAnchors.name: memory
inputAnchors.description: If left empty, a default BufferMemory will be used

inputAnchors.label: Input Moderation
inputAnchors.description: Detect text that could generate harmful output and prevent it from being sent to the language model
inputAnchors.name: inputModeration

inputs.responsePrompt:
You are a smart and friendly AI assistant.

Your job is to help users find information from the database using the given context.

Context: {context}
Chat History: {chat_history}
User Question: {question}

Instructions:

Be Conversational:

Talk like a human (friendly tone).

Do NOT sound like a system.

Always give a helpful answer.

Smart Matching (IMPORTANT):

Do NOT depend only on exact match.

If user says "mark21", try to match similar values like:

mark

mark 21

mark21 (case insensitive)

Even partial match is OK if it is closest.

Data Usage:

Use ONLY the given context.

Do NOT invent new data.

Field Filtering:

Return ONLY what the user asked.

Do NOT return full JSON.

Do NOT include _id, _rev or technical fields.

If Data Not Clearly Found:

Try your best to find closest match from context.

If still not found, say politely:
"I couldn’t find an exact match, but here’s the closest information I found."

Answer Style:

Always respond in sentence format.

Keep it simple and clean.

Be slightly engaging (like a real chat assistant).

Examples:

User: get mark21 data
Answer: I couldn’t find an exact match for "mark21", but I found a similar record for Mark. Mark is 28 years old and lives in Chennai. Do you want more details about him?

User: delip age
Answer: Delip's age is 25 🙂 Do you also need his mobile number?

User: mobile number of arun
Answer: Arun's mobile number is 9539471537 👍 Anything else you'd like to check?

Final Rule:

Always try to give an answer.

Never directly say "Not found" unless absolutely no data exists.

If partial data is available, return that instead of failing.

outputAnchors.name: conversationalRetrievalQAChain
outputAnchors.label: ConversationalRetrievalQAChain
outputAnchors.description: Document QA - built on RetrievalQAChain to provide a chat history component

inputParams.label: Input Variables
inputParams.name: functionInputVariables
inputParams.description: Input variables can be used in the function with prefix $. For example: $var

inputParams.label: Javascript Function
inputParams.name: javascriptFunction
inputParams.description: Must return an array of document objects containing metadata and pageContent if "Document" is selected in the output. If "Text" is selected in the output, it must return a string.

outputAnchors.name: output
outputAnchors.label: Output
outputAnchors.description: Array of document objects containing metadata and pageContent

options.name: document
options.label: Document
options.description: Array of document objects containing metadata and pageContent

options.name: text
options.label: Text
options.description: Concatenated string from pageContent of documents

data.label: Postgres Record Manager
data.name: postgresRecordManager
data.description: Use Postgres to keep track of document writes into the vector databases

inputParams.label: SSL
inputParams.name: ssl
inputParams.description: Use SSL to connect to Postgres

inputParams.label: Namespace
inputParams.name: namespace
inputParams.description: If not specified, chatflowid will be used

inputParams.label: Cleanup
inputParams.name: cleanup
inputParams.description: Read more on the difference between different cleanup methods here

options.label: None
options.name: none
options.description: No clean up of old content

options.label: Incremental
options.name: incremental
options.description: Delete previous versions of the content if content of the source document has changed. Important!! SourceId Key must be specified and document metadata must contains the specified key

options.label: Full
options.name: full
options.description: Same as incremental, but if the source document has been deleted, it will be deleted from vector store as well, incremental mode will not.

inputParams.label: SourceId Key
inputParams.name: sourceIdKey
inputParams.description: Key used to get the true source of document, to be compared against the record. Document metadata must contains SourceId Key

outputAnchors.name: postgresRecordManager
outputAnchors.label: Postgres RecordManager
outputAnchors.description: Use Postgres to keep track of document writes into the vector databases

data.label: Cohere Embeddings
data.name: cohereEmbeddings
data.description: Cohere API to generate embeddings for a given text

inputParams.label: Type
inputParams.name: inputType
inputParams.description: Specifies the type of input passed to the model. Required for embedding models v3 and higher. Official Docs

options.label: search_document
options.name: search_document
options.description: Use this to encode documents for embeddings that you store in a vector database for search use-cases

options.label: search_query
options.name: search_query
options.description: Use this when you query your vector DB to find relevant documents.

options.label: classification
options.name: classification
options.description: Use this when you use the embeddings as an input to a text classifier

options.label: clustering
options.name: clustering
options.description: Use this when you want to cluster the embeddings.

outputAnchors.name: cohereEmbeddings
outputAnchors.label: CohereEmbeddings
outputAnchors.description: Cohere API to generate embeddings for a given text

data.label: Buffer Window Memory
data.name: bufferWindowMemory
data.description: Uses a window of size k to surface the last k back-and-forth to use as memory

inputParams.label: Size
inputParams.name: k
inputParams.description: Window of size k to surface the last k back-and-forth to use as memory.

inputParams.label: Session Id
inputParams.name: sessionId
inputParams.description: If not specified, a random id will be used. Learn more

outputAnchors.name: bufferWindowMemory
outputAnchors.label: BufferWindowMemory
outputAnchors.description: Uses a window of size k to surface the last k back-and-forth to use as memory

data.label: Pinecone
data.name: pinecone
data.description: Upsert embedded data and perform similarity or mmr search using Pinecone, a leading fully managed hosted vector database

inputParams.label: File Upload
inputParams.name: fileUpload
inputParams.description: Allow file upload on the chat

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
**File Upload**

This allows file upload on the chat. Uploaded files will be upserted on the fly to the vector store.

**Note:**
- You can only turn on file upload for one vector store at a time.
- At least one Document Loader node should be connected to the document input.
- Document Loader should be file types like PDF, DOCX, TXT, etc.

**How it works**
- Uploaded files will have the metadata updated with the chatId.
- This will allow the file to be associated with the chatId.
- When querying, metadata will be filtered by chatId to retrieve files associated with the chatId.

inputParams.label: Pinecone Text Key
inputParams.name: pineconeTextKey
inputParams.description: The key in the metadata for storing text. Default to `text`

inputParams.label: Top K
inputParams.name: topK
inputParams.description: Number of top results to fetch. Default to 4

inputParams.label: Fetch K (for MMR Search)
inputParams.name: fetchK
inputParams.description: Number of initial documents to fetch for MMR reranking. Default to 20. Used only when the search type is MMR

inputParams.label: Lambda (for MMR Search)
inputParams.name: lambda
inputParams.description: Number between 0 and 1 that determines the degree of diversity among the results, where 0 corresponds to maximum diversity and 1 to minimum diversity. Used only when the search type is MMR

inputAnchors.label: Record Manager
inputAnchors.name: recordManager
inputAnchors.description: Keep track of the record to prevent duplication

data.name: groqChat
